headline: Undefeated LSU hosts desperate Georgia in SEC showdown
subhead: The Bulldogs need a statement win to salvage their season against the nation's second-ranked Tigers.

xFactor.why: Georgia's ability to disrupt LSU's passing attack and force mistakes will determine if they can keep pace with the Tigers' balanced offense.

keyMatchup: LSU's tempo-setting offense against Georgia's secondary, which has allowed opponents to hit explosive plays in losses to better talent.
body: Georgia's narrow victory over Miami showed the Bulldogs still possess offensive capability, but a 1-3 record is not sustainable for a program of this pedigree. LSU arrives undefeated with a methodical win over Penn State that highlighted the coaching staff's control—they didn't force the issue and let their defense do real work. The gap in records obscures something important: Georgia has played tougher competition and simply hasn't closed moments when it mattered.

LSU's defense ranks among the conference's most disruptive units, and that's where Georgia's makeshift offensive line will face its sternest test. If the Bulldogs' quarterback can manage the pocket and limit negative plays while leaning on their ground game, they stay competitive. But LSU's unbeaten status and home-field advantage suggest the Tigers will control the tempo and lean on the very discipline that got them to 4-0 in the first place.
